figures.caption: Fig. 3. Qualitative comparison on a 7-DoF arm. (a) Baseline (unnormalised actions + Num-IK) shows irregular steps and joint-limit configurations. (b) Proposed bridge (normalised 26-neighbour + TP–DLS) equalises step size and keeps joints away from limits.
Fig. 2. Overall architecture of the proposed bridging framework combining discrete Q-learning planning, geometric regularization, and continuous TP-DLS execution.
Table 1. Three Planners Under Different Obstacle Densities

Fig. 1. Conceptual motivation. Left: classical planning - feasible collision-free path (Q1); Right: practical deployment - high-quality path (Q2). The proposed method meets the second requirement by bridging a voxel RL planner with a continuous execution layer on a redundant manipulator.
